instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-with-context` that asserts an expression is non-nil and, on failure, signals an error that includes the failing form plus a user-supplied context string.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (NUMBERP X)))
            (UNLESS #:G1
              (ERROR "Assertion failed: ~S~%Context: ~A" '(NUMBERP X)
                     "x must be a number"))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (NUMBERP X)))
            (UNLESS #:G112
              (ERROR "Assertion failed: ~S~%Context: ~A" '(NUMBERP X)
                     "x must be a number"))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
